architecture.summary: GoT models reasoning as a graph rather than a linear chain. The system represents thought units as nodes and connections as edges. It uses a two-stage framework with a dedicated GoT encoder that learns graph representations from thought units. The graph representation is fused with the original input representation via a gated fusion mechanism, allowing the model to capture non-sequential aspects of human reasoning before feeding the combined representation into the downstream task model.
trained_components: GoT encoder for thought graph representation

biblio.title: GoT: Effective Graph-of-Thought Reasoning in Language Models

claims.statement: GoT achieves significant improvement over the strong CoT baseline on the AQUA-RAT test set
GoT boosts accuracy from 85.19% to 87.59% using the T5-base model over the state-of-the-art Multimodal-CoT on the ScienceQA test set
Graph-of-Thought reasoning models human thought processes not only as a chain but also as a graph, capturing the non-sequential nature of human thinking
